lightBloom.note: The Ferrari-red top banner should bleed light downward onto the telemetry strips below — like stadium floodlights hitting the pit wall monitors
Purple fastest lap glow should be DRAMATIC — bloom spreads 14px and shifts the entire bottom-right ambient color to purple momentarily
DRS lamps at #00FF88 should bloom aggressively — these are LED indicators at close range

lensFlare.note: Green flag has a short horizontal lens streak — like camera capturing LED signage

chromaticAberration.note: Edges-only is specified. Good. But during the intro_wipe (frames 0-45), temporarily increase to 2.5px across the full frame — broadcast camera zoom-in motion artifact

filmGrain.note: 0.02 at 15fps is specified. Add broadcast interlace artifact: every other frame, offset all scan lines 0.5px downward — simulates deinterlaced broadcast footage

motionBlur.note: Dots racing around the track need directional motion blur along their travel direction — they represent 300+ km/h objects
Scrolling P&L ticker text should have subtle horizontal motion blur — broadcast crawl effect

reflectionGlare.note: Pit wall monitors are outdoor LCD screens — faint sun reflection from the paddock

particleEffects.note: No particles needed — this is a clean broadcast environment. Instead, add subtle moiré pattern on the carbon-fiber background texture that shifts with camera drift

cameraDrift.note: Minimal drift — broadcast cameras are stabilized. But add very occasional micro-vibration (2 frames, 1px) when the pit crew runs past — mechanical floor vibration

dramaticReveals.note: Gap times should rapidly count up from +0.000 to their actual values — like the timing tower populating after a sector split
Staggered fade-in at frames 90-120 is specified. Add color flash: each cell briefly flashes white before revealing its final color (purple/green/yellow) — the split just came in

breathingAnimations.note: Speed trace lines should have continuous micro-variation even when data is static — real telemetry is never perfectly smooth
RPM bar segments should shimmer rapidly — they represent an engine screaming at 15,000 RPM

transitionMoments.description: Pit stop sequence — pit_strategy_timeline marker drops with bounce, speed graph for one agent flatlines momentarily (pit entry), gap timer flashes as intervals change, radio transcript gets 'BOX BOX BOX' message

Fastest lap moment — purple banner pulses with bloom, sector times for Alpha all flash purple simultaneously, gap_timer bestLap value updates with white flash, track dot for Alpha briefly trails purple sparks

dataDensityGaps.fix: This is 432px of reserved space with only constructor_points using 48px. Fill with: LAP-BY-LAP delta chart (mini sparklines per agent), TIRE AGE counter per agent, and WEATHER FORECAST timeline showing rain probability over next 10 laps
Add G-FORCE readout (lateral + longitudinal) per agent, and a mini speed comparison overlay — ghost trace of the leader's speed on each agent's graph
Add DRS detection zone activation indicator (flashing green segment when gap < 1s), and mini sector time text at each sector marker
Add TRACK EVOLUTION indicator: rubber on racing line % (grip level) and track temperature trend arrow
Add TIRE COMPOUND icon (colored circle) and PIT STOPS count column

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: High-contrast broadcast look — deep blacks (#050508), punchy accent colors. Grade like the official F1 broadcast with slightly crushed blacks and lifted highlights. The carbon-fiber background should have a very subtle blue sheen (#0A0A14)

keyMoments.shift: Purple color wash across the entire bottom third of the frame — #A020F0 at 5% opacity overlay. This is the signature F1 purple moment
Entire frame shifts warm — background becomes #12120A, all non-essential elements dim 20%, yellow banner dominates

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: Four agent colors are distinct and well-chosen. But the tire compound colors (HARD white, MEDIUM yellow, SOFT red) need more prominence — they should appear as colored dots/badges throughout, not just in the pit strategy timeline
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Carbon-fiber weave texture is excellent. Add very subtle vignette with a warm lower edge — monitor desk reflection from below

typographyNotes.fonts: Titillium Web is the actual F1 typeface family — perfect choice. 'F1 Wide' for the primary is authentic but needs a fallback that matches the extreme width
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: JetBrains Mono for data is good but consider using Titillium Web Semibold with tabular figures for gap times — keeps the visual unity
typographyNotes.displayFont: The hero timer at 72px is appropriately large. But LAP counter at 28px could be bigger (36px) — it is critical race information
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 11px data labels are borderline. The 9px chart axis labels should be 10px. Add more visual separation between panel titles (14px) and data labels (11px) — they are too close in size

continuousAmbience.sound: V6 turbo-hybrid engine tone modulated by speed trace — higher pitch at higher values, lower in braking zones. Four engines audible, slightly offset
Crowd roar as ambient bed — rises and falls with on-track action

eventSounds.sound: Three ascending electronic tones (C5-E5-G5) — the F1 timing jingle
Radio squelch + team radio voice — compressed, slightly distorted as in real broadcasts
Sharp whoosh + crowd surge — the iconic DRS overtake sound moment
Impact wrench staccato burst (BRRRRT) — 2-second pit stop audio

silenceMoments.description: Brief audio dip before the overtake gap flash — the director cuts to onboard, wind noise drops, then the overtake happens with full audio surge
